title: Count Numbers with Unique Digits
hints: Consider the count for small n. Notice that for n=1, all 10 digits are valid. For n=2, you pick the first digit, then any different second digit.
This is a permutation problem. For n>1, the first digit has 9 choices (1-9), and each subsequent position has fewer choices based on previous picks.
Use the product rule: 9 * 9 * 8 * ... * (11-n). Handle n=0 and n=1 as special cases since the pattern changes slightly.
